methods.description: This method involves conducting sequence alignments, estimating genetic divergence, selecting models of nucleotide substitution, and performing phylogenetic reconstructions to understand evolutionary relationships.

steps.description: Sequence alignments were conducted in MAFFT alignment software (version 7.058) with default settings.
Estimates of uncorrected genetic divergence (p-distance) for sequence pairs were conducted in MEGA (version 5.2.2).
Statistical selection of models of nucleotide substitution in jModelTest (version 2.1.4).
steps.searchable_summary: select nucleotide substitution model using jModelTest

steps.description: The gene dataset was tested for nucleotide substitution saturation using the test by Xia et al. (2003) implemented in DAMBE v. 5.3.48.
steps.searchable_summary: test for nucleotide substitution saturation using DAMBE

steps.description: Performed four phylogenetic reconstructions: two by Bayesian inference, one by maximum likelihood, and one by parsimony.
steps.searchable_summary: perform phylogenetic reconstructions using Bayesian inference, maximum likelihood, and parsimony

steps.description: A consensus tree of the two Bayesian and maximum likelihood analyses was constructed by 50% majority-rule in the Mesquite package.

methods.description: This method involves analyzing adult morphological characters of South American species of Palaemon to verify morphological support for phylogenetic clades and provide new diagnoses.

steps.description: Checked diagnostic characters traditionally used to differentiate P. carteri from P. ivonicus.
Analyzed other characters traditionally used in Palaemonidae and others less explored.
Listed pairwise differences between specimens from each clade and validated those with more than 80% constancy.
steps.searchable_summary: validate morphological differences with constancy check

steps.description: Verified diagnostic characters in original descriptions and type series.
steps.searchable_summary: verify diagnostic characters in original descriptions

methods.description: This method involves DNA extraction, amplification, and sequencing to obtain molecular data for phylogenetic analysis.

steps.description: Total genomic DNA was extracted from the muscle tissue of the abdomen.
steps.searchable_summary: extract genomic DNA from muscle tissue

steps.description: Amplified a 550 bp region of the mitochondrial 16S rRNA gene using PCR.
PCR products were purified using Sure Clean.
Sequenced the purified DNA with the ABI Big Dye® Terminator Mix.
steps.searchable_summary: sequence DNA using ABI Big Dye Terminator Mix
